Like, you know, I don't know if that's what you were looking for.
Absolutely.
That's one of those, like, yeah, it just, it grinds my gears.
So yeah, that's really good to know, because that's something I wouldn't necessarily know or think of as like a non educator.
And it totally makes sense.
Yeah, that's, that's really useful.
Yeah, and then just lastly, unless anyone, unless like, Maureen, if there's anything else.

Do you want to add on to, like, you know, we have time on our end, but do you have any, like, additional tips or anything you want to add in terms of, like, ensuring any of this, like, more professional content aligns with, like, real classroom challenges and, like, teacher priorities that we haven't already covered or anything else you want to add?
I don't think so.

I think, you know, the biggest thing, I mean, our mission kind of drives it, which is, like, the idea of making school awesome every day for students and the people who support them by finding out what they need and making it happen.
Like, that is, that's our mission at School AI, and it is, I feel, truly encompassing of the work that educators are doing.

Like, we are trying to, every day we go in, we have our group of kids, and we're trying to make sure that they know the content that we're teaching them, that they're enjoying learning about it, that it's facilitating this ongoing, you know, passion for learning and developing them as, you know, learning.
Learners and human beings, and we need to figure out what each of them needs.
So I think those are the big ideas behind like the challenges.
Like every challenge kind of is attributed back to that.
Like it's making it fun and engaging and finding out what the kids need.
Like every single challenge that educators face kind of comes back to those big principles and those ideas.
So yeah, that's what I would say is that's how I make it relevant to educators is to really help them understand that like the reason you got into education is to connect with kids.
It's to teach them and show them your passion.
And this is how we're going to do it.
This is how we're going to get that time back.
This is how we're going to get them more engaged, get them the personalized help that they need.
Like that's what we're trying to do at School AI.
So yeah.
I feel like I just got a soapbox there.
Sorry.
No, that was amazing.
I was telling you.
Nikki, that like this, the showcase was so good for, for the soapbox thing, but I got all misty high.
I was like, this is beautiful.
It was so good.
No, this is really, this is really amazing.
I'm so glad we had a chance to talk.
And yeah, just to give you some like additional context of like what we're going to do with this.

So after this call, like I'll send you a link to this call so you can use it internally to kind of like help your team, Nikki, like going forward for like, you know, if you, if you do start that interaction, journal review process, and then our on Megan and my end, we're going to take this call, take the transcript of this call and like pull out the most like salient and useful points of it, which are like very many and like thorough and kind of feed that into our workflow.
Maureen, what we have is like our workflow has like a number of artifacts, like based on like company context, all the way down to like article tone of voice and everything.
And so we can take all these points and build it in.

To that, and then see how it informs our articles going forward, which we won't see until next week's articles, at least, because these ones will be built on the, at least the, most of them will be built on the, like, previous iteration of the, of the artifacts, so, yeah, Megan, was there anything else you wanted to add before we sign off?
I know Nikki will be seeing you in another couple hours, as well.
Yeah, again, you know, that's, yeah, that's, that's it for me again, and this has been super helpful, so, yeah, appreciate you, you all taking the time.
Awesome, well, it great meeting both of you, you all, and Nikki, sorry, I'm exaggerating, so, I love it.

No, we love, we love School AI, and, yeah, just so happy to get to meet another teammate, and we, you know, Nikki says names, she'll mention people, and then she's like, oh, yeah, this person does this, this person does this, and then he's like, and then, like, I saw a bunch of people in the showcase, and then I was like, oh, yeah, like, I remember that name, and I saw Graham, and I was like, oh, that's Graham, so it's just, like, yeah, it's just, it's just so great.
To meet more people on the team anytime we can.
Awesome.
Great.
We'll talk to you soon.
Thanks a lot.
Yeah, you too.
Ciao.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review and improve SEO content workflow for SchoolAI to better align with educator needs and readability.](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Shift content focus to best teaching practices, pedagogy, and real classroom challenges](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=2003.0)
  - [Improve scannability with descriptive headers, bullet points, and key takeaways at the top](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=1089.0)
  - [Target 900-1100 word count for shorter pieces, balancing SEO needs with educator time constraints](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=1549.0)
  - [Avoid overly technical AI explanations and language that portrays teachers as martyrs/superheroes](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=335.0)

## Topics

### Content Focus and Tone

  - [Lean into best teaching practices, pedagogy, and "why" behind AI tools](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=2003.0)
  - [Avoid over-indexing on technical AI explanations (e.g. how LLMs work)](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=361.0)
  - [Strike balance between formal tone and accessibility (pre-K through higher ed)](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=263.0)
  - [Frame teachers as professionals, not martyrs/superheroes ("making school awesome every day")](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=2065.0)

### Readability and Structure

  - [Improve scannability with descriptive H2/H3 headers (not generic)](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=1089.0)
  - [Use bullet points, numbered sections for visual flow](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=1687.0)
  - [Limit text under headers to \~2 paragraphs max](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=1687.0)
  - [Consider "read more" expandable sections to avoid overwhelming readers](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=1179.0)
  - [Move key takeaways to top of articles (currently at bottom)](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=1172.0)

### Word Count and SEO

  - [Target 900-1100 words for shorter pieces (including key takeaways)](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=1549.0)
  - [Balance SEO needs with educator time constraints](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=1496.0)
  - [Maintain content pyramids (broad topics -\> granular supporting pieces)](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=924.0)

### Educator-Friendly Resources

  - [Create AI terminology glossary page (link terms in articles)](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=1269.0)
  - [Emulate Edutopia's educator-friendly format (short, chunked content)](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=1409.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Jenn/Megan: Update content workflow artifacts with meeting insights](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=2115.0)
  - [Experiment with key takeaways at top of articles (A/B test)](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=1179.0)
  - [Nikki: Consider design/formatting changes to improve readability](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=1620.0)
  - [Maureen: Review broader "AI for middle school teachers" piece for feedback](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=1030.0)
  - [Team: Continually refine balance between SEO needs and educator-friendly content](https://fathom.video/share/utKKRTDkjxwww-K7qyCTDXFYmaSovEoC?tab=summary&timestamp=2065.0)

action_items.description: Adjust workflow to ensure headers are actionable, connect w/ readers, stand out, good for scannability
Pass on to Addy to start putting key takeaways at top of articles from today's publishing/staging
Update workflow to target 900-1100 word count incl. key takeaways for shorter pieces
Modify workflow to avoid language implying teacher martyrdom/superhero status; focus on professionalism
Send meeting recording link to Nikki for internal team use
Update workflow artifacts w/ meeting insights (company context, article tone, etc.) for next week's articles
Pull out salient points from meeting transcript to feed into workflow
